Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a classification for properties, helping to organize and filter them (e.g., Residential, Commercial).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

name.description: The name of the property category (e.g., 'Residential', 'Commercial', 'Land').

description.description: A brief explanation or context for this property category.

Property.title: Property
Property.description: Manages details for both finished and under-construction properties, including core information and specific attributes based on its lifecycle stage.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Property entity.

name.description: The common name given to the property.

code.description: A unique identifier code for the property, distinct from its primary key.

location.description: The physical location or address of the property.

size.description: The total size of the property, typically in square meters or square feet.

description.description: A detailed description providing more information about the property.

propertyType.description: Indicates the current stage of the property (e.g., 'Finished', 'UnderConstruction').

dateCreated.description: The date and time when the property record was created.

isDeleted.description: A flag indicating if the property record has been soft-deleted (true) or is active (false).

status.description: The occupancy status for finished properties (e.g., 'Occupied', 'Vacant').

monthlyRent.description: The expected monthly rental income for finished properties.

paymentDueDay.description: The day of the month (1-31) when rent payments are typically due for finished properties.

tenantName.description: The name of the current tenant, if the finished property is occupied.

tenantContactDetails.description: Contact information for the current tenant (e.g., email, phone number).

totalInvestmentCost.description: The total investment cost for the property, typically set when construction is complete and used as a baseline for investment recovery and profitability calculations.

constructionStage.description: The current stage of construction for under-construction properties (e.g., 'Foundation', 'Roofing', 'Finishing').

estimatedBudget.description: An optional estimated budget for under-construction properties.

ConstructionExpense.title: ConstructionExpense
ConstructionExpense.description: Tracks individual expenses incurred during the construction phase of a property.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ConstructionExpense entity.

itemName.description: The name or description of the item or service purchased.

quantity.description: The quantity of the item purchased.

unitPrice.description: The price per unit of the item.

totalPrice.description: The total cost for the item (quantity * unit price), auto-calculated but stored.

vendor.description: The vendor or supplier from whom the item was purchased.

datePurchased.description: The date when the item was purchased.

notes.description: Any additional notes or details about the expense.

receiptUri.description: A URI pointing to an uploaded receipt for the expense.

sentimentAnalysisAlert.description: A flag indicating if the sentiment analysis tool detected potential unexpected cost overruns related to this expense.

RentalIncome.title: RentalIncome
RentalIncome.description: Records all rental payments received for finished properties, tracking their status and details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the RentalIncome entity.

tenantName.description: The name of the tenant who made the payment.

paymentDate.description: The date when the payment was received.

dueDate.description: The date when the payment was originally due.

paymentMethod.description: The method used for the payment (e.g., 'Bank Transfer', 'Cash', 'Credit Card').

paymentStatus.description: The current status of the payment (e.g., 'Paid', 'Pending', 'Overdue').

MaintenanceExpense.title: MaintenanceExpense
MaintenanceExpense.description: Tracks ongoing expenses related to maintenance and general upkeep of finished properties.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MaintenanceExpense entity.

expenseType.description: The category of the maintenance expense (e.g., 'Repair', 'Utility', 'Cleaning').

description.description: A description of the maintenance expense incurred.

amount.description: The total amount of the maintenance expense.

date.description: The date when the maintenance expense was incurred.

vendor.description: The vendor or service provider for the maintenance work.

receiptUri.description: A URI pointing to an uploaded receipt for the maintenance expense.

definition.description: Top-level collection for property categories. Access: Read-only for all authenticated users, Write access restricted to global 'admin' role.
Collection for assigning global 'admin' roles. A document's existence at `/roles_admin/{uid}` grants admin privileges to that user. Access: Read by current user, Write by global 'admin' role.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user assigned the 'admin' role.

definition.description: Collection for assigning global 'accountant' roles. A document's existence at `/roles_accountant/{uid}` grants accountant privileges to that user. Access: Read by current user, Write by global 'admin' role.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user assigned the 'accountant' role.

definition.description: Collection for assigning global 'viewer' roles. A document's existence at `/roles_viewer/{uid}` grants viewer privileges to that user. Access: Read by current user, Write by global 'admin' role.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user assigned the 'viewer' role.

definition.description: Collection for individual properties. Includes a denormalized 'members' map (`members: { [uid: string]: 'admin' | 'manager' | 'viewer' }`) for property-specific authorization, defining who can access and manage this property and its subcollections. Access is determined by the `members` map, supporting different roles per property.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique ID of the property.

definition.description: Subcollection under a specific property to track construction expenses. Includes a denormalized 'propertyMembers' map for authorization independence, copied from its parent Property's 'members' map. This allows for security rules to validate access without reading the parent property document.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent property.

params.name: expenseId
params.description: The unique ID of the construction expense.

definition.description: Subcollection under a specific property to record rental income. Includes a denormalized 'propertyMembers' map for authorization independence, copied from its parent Property's 'members' map. This ensures security rules can validate access without reading the parent property document.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent property.

params.name: incomeId
params.description: The unique ID of the rental income record.

definition.description: Subcollection under a specific property to track maintenance expenses. Includes a denormalized 'propertyMembers' map for authorization independence, copied from its parent Property's 'members' map. This allows for security rules to validate access without reading the parent property document.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent property.

params.name: expenseId
params.description: The unique ID of the maintenance expense.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and highly debuggable by strictly adhering to the core design principles and mandates. The central pillar of this design is **Authorization Independence** through aggressive denormalization, specifically for property-related access. Each `Property` document includes a `members` map (e.g., `members: { 'uid1': 'admin', 'uid2': 'manager' }`) which explicitly defines which users have what role for that specific property. To support atomic operations and prevent the need for `get()` calls in security rules, this `members` map is further denormalized as `propertyMembers` into every document in its subcollections (`ConstructionExpense`, `RentalIncome`, `MaintenanceExpense`). This means a `ConstructionExpense` document, for instance, will contain `propertyMembers` directly, allowing security rules to authorize access solely based on the document's own data and `request.auth.uid`, making rules simple and performant.

**Querying for Authorized Properties (QAPs):** For `list` operations, this denormalization strategy enables efficient and secure queries. Users can directly query the `/properties` collection with a `where()` clause, such as `db.collection('properties').where('members.' + request.auth.uid, 'in', ['admin', 'manager', 'viewer'])`. This allows Firestore to efficiently filter documents where the authenticated user is listed in the `members` map, ensuring that rules are not used as filters (QAPs).

**Structural Segregation** is maintained by creating dedicated top-level collections for global roles (e.g., `/roles_admin`, `/roles_accountant`, `/roles_viewer`). These collections store the `uid` of users possessing these global roles, enabling easy existence checks in security rules. All documents within a collection, such as `/properties/{propertyId}/constructionExpenses`, share the same security posture, as their authorization is consistently derived from the denormalized `propertyMembers` map within each document. This homogeneity simplifies security rules significantly.

**Hierarchical Paths** (`/properties/{propertyId}/constructionExpenses/{expenseId}`) are utilized for entities that are strongly related to a parent `Property`, making the data organization logical and access control intuitive. The wildcards (`{propertyId}`, `{expenseId}`, etc.) are descriptive, improving data clarity and predictability. This comprehensive approach minimizes complexity, enhances security rule robustness, and ensures a scalable and maintainable Firestore database for Property Pulse.
